free_text: Activation cross sections of deuteron induced reactions
on silver in the 33-50 MeV energy range
Natural silver target, thicknesses: 10 um
The stack was irradiated at an external beam line of
the Cyclone 90 cyclotron of the Universite Catholique
in Louvain la Neuve (LLN) for 40 min with a 50 MeV,
50 nA deuteron beam. The stack contained a sequence of
10 blocks of Al (10 um), Sr(NO3)2 (3 um), Al (50 um),
Er(32 um), Al (10 um), Ba(NO2) (2 um, sedimented),
Al (50 um) and Ag(10 um) foils.
The 10 Ag targets covered the 50-33 MeV energy range.
Nondestructive measurement without chemical separation.
Energy losses in the sample foils and stopping powers
were calculated using the SRIM-2003 code.
Four series of spectra were recorded.
Decay data were taken from NuDat database.
Chapter 4
It was not specified.
The combined uncertainty on cross-section was
estimated
number of target nuclei and non-uniformity(1%);
beam current (7%);
detector efficiency (5%)
beam loss correction (1.5%)
statistical error (1-20%)
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
The measured experimental data were compared to
and analyzed with theoretical calculations based on
the code TALYS, ALICE-D, EMPIRE-D and Systematics.
On the basis of the new experimental cross
sections integral yields were calculated and compared
to experimental yields taken from the literature.
